headline: Japan spent $74B defending the yen - real battle is with the Fed
detail: Yen intervention on this scale signals serious currency stress. If the dollar surges or risk-off sentiment spreads, tech and growth names - exactly your book - are the first to get hit. Leveraged positions like SOXL and SPAL get hit hardest and fastest.
rec: No trade triggered, but this is the macro risk most likely to crack your book overnight. Watch dollar strength tomorrow morning before adding anything new.

headline: Oil wobbles as U.S.-Iran talks break down
detail: Oil uncertainty feeds into broader risk sentiment and can spike inflation fears, which pressures the Fed-rate narrative that growth/tech stocks depend on. Not a direct chip catalyst, but sustained oil volatility historically bleeds into tech selling.
rec: No action needed today, but if oil spikes sharply higher on this news, treat it as a yellow flag for your SMH and QQQ positions and check your stops.

headline: LNG disruption may continue for months as top producer withholds shipments
detail: Energy supply disruption can feed inflation prints, which complicates the Fed's rate path. A hawkish Fed surprise is one of the cleaner ways to knock high-multiple tech names lower. Your whole book is exposed to this indirectly.
rec: ERY (energy bear inverse play) shows a 7/12 scanner setup with entry $12.93-12.95 and stop $12.88 - if you want a small hedge against energy-driven risk-off, this is the only scanner-backed idea available. Tiny size only given your tech concentration and margin.

headline: Employers who laid off workers citing AI are starting to regret it
detail: This is a sentiment data point that cuts against the AI-spend-at-all-costs narrative. If the market starts questioning AI productivity ROI, CRWD and the chip names powering AI infrastructure could face multiple compression. Not a crisis today, but worth watching as a trend.
rec: No action today - one anecdotal headline doesn't move the needle. But CRWD (+6.07%) is your biggest single-stock winner; if AI sentiment softens over coming weeks, that's where you'd trim first.
